page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 physical cores of the AWS Graviton processor family using the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz with a dedicated 1:1 core-to-thread allocation, the system features 512 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding 8 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations show strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core tests and software compilation via Clang scoring in the top 25 percent. Small-block cached memory read bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 3792.89 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression. The server demonstrates strong throughput for database and web operations, achieving 74.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 47.45 GB/sec in static web serving. Conversely, LLM inference speeds are weak to poor, particularly for text generation tasks.

The ARM-based architecture and bare-metal deployment model offer high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads without hypervisor overhead. The combination of 512 GB RAM, dedicated physical cores, and 3.8 TB of local NVMe SSD storage makes this server type suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, static web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical ARM64 cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in software compilation, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, alongside high small-block cached memory read bandwidth. However, single-core processing and LLM inference speeds are weaker. The combination of high memory capacity, dedicated physical cores, and fast local NVMe storage makes this server type highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 ARM64 cores, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 ARM64 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3.8 TB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 server combining 512 GB of memory with 3.8 TB of local NVMe storage for high-throughput database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz
512 GB DDR4 memory providing 8 GB of RAM per core
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and Redis operations
